description.value: A DDD-flavored, opinionated agentic harness for Claude Code that turns a raw idea into a modeled backlog of bounded contexts, then into parallel, dependency-aware execution.

website.comment: No dedicated website; the GitHub repository serves as the project home (repo homepage field is null).

creator.comment: GitHub user heimeshoff; no organization affiliation stated.

first-release.comment: Repo created 2026-04-24; first tagged release v0.1.0 on 2026-05-14.

pricing-model.comment: Free and open source (Apache-2.0); no commercial tier.

covered-phases.comment: Five skills map to phases: Brainstorm (discovery: vision + context map), Modeling (architecture: bounded contexts, aggregates, events; and tasks), Capture/Modeling backlog + dependency DAG (planning, tasks), Work (implementation with TDD red-green-refactor, so testing), and a verifier gate that audits worker code before commit (review). Research skill also contributes to discovery.

methodology-style.comment: DDD domain model / bounded-context backlog is the central driving artifact (spec-centric); an orchestrator routes to specialist agents incl. a verifier and research-reviewer (multi-agent); idea -> modeled backlog -> execution (plan-and-execute); Work skill enforces TDD red-green-refactor (strict-tdd).

opinionated.comment: Self-described as an 'opinionated' harness with a prescribed brainstorm -> model -> work loop.

ceremony-level.comment: DDD bounded-context modeling, per-context READMEs/INDEXes, ADRs, a protocol diary, and verifier/research gates make the default workflow document- and gate-heavy, though a lightweight Capture path exists for quick intake.

mechanization-level.comment: Skills auto-trigger on natural-language phrasing and workers execute code and commit end-to-end; not a fully managed hosted pipeline (runs inside local Claude Code).

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Explicitly human-in-the-loop: 'you think out loud, the harness captures and models, and only then does it write code.' Plus agent verifier/research-reviewer gates before commits/reports.

approval-gates.comment: Interpretive: the human owns the conversational modeling (spec) and backlog refinement (plan) before autonomous work begins. The verifier gate before each commit is an agent (agent-review), not human approval.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Deliberately not idea-to-merge autonomous: modeling requires human dialogue. Once a backlog is modeled, parallel workers can execute it dependency-aware, but the framework is designed around the human-in-the-loop gate.

autonomy-level.comment: Phase-level: human drives brainstorm/modeling, then workers execute the modeled backlog autonomously with an agent verifier gate before each commit.

rollback-support.comment: No dedicated undo/rollback command documented. One-task-one-commit is a deliberate convention that facilitates manual git revert, but that is git, not a framework feature.

supported-agents.comment: Distributed and run as a Claude Code plugin; explicitly coupled to Claude Code.

editor-integrations.comment: Runs in Claude Code (terminal CLI); ships an optional VS Code bridge extension to seed sessions, plus a local read-only web dashboard (Node-stdlib server).

model-agnostic.comment: Locked to Claude Code / Anthropic Claude as the runtime.

mcp-support.comment: No Model Context Protocol integration documented in the README.

artifact-formats.comment: Vision, context maps, ADRs, research reports and tasks are Markdown; task files carry YAML frontmatter (id, status, depends_on, type).

artifacts-covered.comment: Produces vision + context maps and per-bounded-context domain docs (architecture), ADRs under knowledge/decisions (decisions), a dependency-ordered backlog (plans), and task markdown files (tasks). Also a chronological protocol log and verified research reports, which have no dedicated tag.

project-layout.value: All state lives under a per-project .agentheim/ directory: vision.md, context-map.md, contexts/<bounded-context>/ (README.md, INDEX.md, backlog/todo/doing/done task folders, concepts/), and knowledge/ (index.md, protocol.md, decisions/, research/).

artifacts-in-git.comment: All artifacts live in-repo under .agentheim/ and are committed (one commit per completed task).

language-agnostic.comment: Artifacts are language-neutral markdown; workers execute code in any language. The harness itself is JavaScript.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Skills auto-trigger via natural-language phrasing (instructional); Work enforces TDD red-green-refactor (test-gates); a fresh-context verifier audits worker code before commit and a research-reviewer re-verifies claims before publication (agent-review).

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Task execution respects a depends_on dependency DAG, but the brainstorm -> model -> work phases are not hard-gated: Capture and other skills can be entered directly.

artifact-validation.comment: Tasks use structured YAML frontmatter and INDEX files are auto-maintained, but no explicit schema validation of artifacts is documented.

memory-implementation.comment: A 'memory layer' of per-BC INDEX.md files and a top-level knowledge/index.md, maintained incrementally and consulted for prior-art lookup and dependency hints.

persistent-memory.comment: knowledge/ indexes, protocol log, decisions and per-BC docs persist in-repo across sessions with no manual handoff.

project-context-files.comment: Produces and maintains project-scoped context files under .agentheim/ (vision.md, INDEX.md, knowledge/index.md).

cost-tracking.comment: No token or cost tracking features documented.

run-history.comment: knowledge/protocol.md is a chronological diary (newest on top) recording what happened across the workflow.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collection is documented; as a local OSS Claude Code plugin there is nothing to disclose or opt out of, so the attribute is not meaningfully applicable.

custom-templates.comment: No user-facing artifact/prompt template customization documented.

hooks.comment: No user-defined lifecycle hooks documented for Agentheim itself.

sub-agents.comment: Ships fixed specialist agents/skills (modeler, architect, researcher, worker, verifier); no documented mechanism for users to define their own roles or skills.

marketplace.comment: Distributed via the Claude Code plugin marketplace, but there is no marketplace or community repository of shared Agentheim templates/skills.

install-mechanism.comment: Installed as a Claude Code plugin (/plugin marketplace add heimeshoff/agentheim; /plugin install). Mapped to editor-extension as the closest tag; none of the tags cleanly cover 'Claude Code plugin'.

setup-time.comment: A few /plugin marketplace and /plugin install commands inside Claude Code.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely locally; all state in-repo, dashboard is a Node-stdlib server with no hosted service.

requires-account.comment: Agentheim itself needs no vendor account. Its runtime (Claude Code) requires an Anthropic account, but that is the runtime, not the workflow.

git-worktree-support.comment: Parallel workers claim tasks from the doing/ folder respecting the dependency DAG, but no explicit git worktree usage is documented.

documentation-quality.comment: Detailed README covering install, all five skills, dashboard and VS Code bridge; a visual workflow PDF/HTML; in-repo ADRs; subsystem READMEs; CHANGELOG/RELEASE. No case studies or tutorials beyond the reference repo.

github-stars.comment: 26 stars as of 2026-07-13; young project, count stales quickly.

active-maintenance.comment: Last push 2026-07-09 and release v0.9.1 on 2026-07-09, well within 90 days of the 2026-07-13 research date.

example-projects.comment: No separate example projects or documented case studies; the repository itself is the reference implementation.

community-channel.comment: No dedicated community channel; GitHub Discussions is disabled and no Discord/Slack/forum is listed. Only GitHub issues (2 open).
